sources.notes: Choose erases and splits to maximize the final gcd of all remaining numbers.
Official editorial characterizes when a number can be made divisible by a fixed g: it is good if x is a multiple of g or x>=4g.

annotation.summary: Maximize the gcd of the remaining whiteboard numbers after up to k erases and arbitrary split operations.
annotation.constraints: a_i<=n and total n<=2e5, so every possible gcd g can be checked with frequency prefix sums.
annotation.core_idea: Fix a target gcd g. All multiples of g are already good, and any x>=4g can be split into two written multiples of g by discarding the middle part. Conversely, a non-multiple x<4g cannot be made divisible by g. Thus g is feasible iff count(x>=4g)+freq(g)+freq(2g)+freq(3g) >= n-k. Check all g using frequencies and prefix sums.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case after frequency preprocessing.

solution_variants.name: enumerate-gcd-feasibility
solution_variants.summary: For every g, count values that are already multiples among g,2g,3g or large enough to split into multiples.

tags.evidence: The feasibility condition is entirely about divisibility by a candidate gcd g.
Prefix sums of value frequencies count how many numbers are at least 4g.
For a candidate gcd, every bad number must be erased and every good number can be kept independently.
